name: Luther's Barbecued Ribs-Voltz
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium stainless-steel or enamelware saucepan, melt margarine or butter slowly.
2. Add vinegar, catsup, horseradish, lime or lemon juice, salt, and Worcestershire and hot pepper sauces.
3. Simmer uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes to blend flavors.
4. Use as basting sauce for pork, chicken, or other meats and serve as a table sauce. Leftover sauce can be refrigerated and kept up to a week.
5. Place ribs about 6 inches above hot coals.
6. Brush lightly with sauce and brown on one side.
7. Keep a water bottle handy when using this sauce as it may cause flames to shoot up.
8. Turn, brush again with sauce, and brown the other side.
9. Continue turning and basting every 10 minutes until ribs are done, about 1 hour.
10. Check by cutting near bone in a center section. If juices run clear or golden, the ribs are done.
11. Remove ribs to a platter. Cut into 1- to 3-rib sections and serve with any remaining sauce.
